description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a series of rectangular photovoltaic panels arranged on a sloped platform. The panels are set within a rigid, likely metallic frame, and the surface is divided into distinct sections, some of which are covered with a grid-like pattern indicative of solar cells. The structure includes a raised rear wall, possibly for wind protection or to support the tilt of the panels, and a base that appears to be designed for ground mounting. The platform is labeled with a slope specification, suggesting it is engineered for optimal solar exposure. The overall design is robust, with a combination of smooth and textured surfaces, and the coloration is primarily a mix of black, gray, and light blue tones.
description: A sloped, ground-mounted solar panel array with a raised rear wall and grid-patterned photovoltaic surfaces, primarily in black, gray, and blue.
description_view_0: The sloped platform with grid-patterned solar panels and a prominent rear wall is visible, showing the main surface and structural details.
description_view_1: A side profile highlighting the height of the rear wall and the overall thickness of the structure, with minimal surface detail visible.
description_view_2: A view from the opposite end, showing the length of the array and the arrangement of the panels along the sloped surface.
description_view_3: A rear perspective emphasizing the full width of the raised wall and the sloped platform, with the grid pattern of the panels visible.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25-30 degrees, facing away from the rear wall, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a rigid metallic (likely aluminum or steel) frame and support structure
attribute_array_configuration: single sloped row of approximately 6-8 rectangular panels arranged side by side
